title: Hide Guest Users Section When No Guests Exist
story: As a DataTools Pro account owner, I want the Guest Users section to be hidden on the Account Sharing screen when I have no guest users, so that the interface is clean and not confusing.
description:
## Bug
The Guest Users section was visible on the My Account Sharing screen even when there were no guest users. This occurred after switching from a shared account back to the user's own account — the Guest Users section from the other account's context persisted.

## Fix
Conditionally hide the Guest Users section when no guest users exist for the current account owner.

## Testing
- When Account A invites Account B: Account A sees the Guest Users section with Account B listed and can delete the invite.
- When Account B has not invited anyone: Account B does not see the Guest Users section at all.
acceptance_criteria: Guest Users section is hidden when the account has no guest users
Guest Users section appears with the list when guests exist
Account owner can see and manage their own guest invites
Switching between accounts does not leak guest user sections
ux_changes: Guest Users section is conditionally hidden when no guest users exist for the current account.
middleware_changes: Added conditional check on guest user count before rendering the Guest Users section.
implementation_notes: Follow-up discussion raised the question of adding explicit permissions to grant or deny the ability for guest accounts to invite other users, potentially tied to account licensing.
comment_summary: Developer fixed the visibility issue and provided detailed test scenarios. Reporter raised a follow-up question about adding explicit invite permissions tied to account licensing.
